title: New to Canada Mortgage Programs Explained
metaTitle: New to Canada Mortgage Programs — 2026 Guide
metaDescription: Plain-English guide to Canadian mortgage programs for newcomers and permanent residents. Foreign credit, work-permit income, overseas down payment.
excerpt: Coming to Canada and ready to buy? Three insurer programs accept foreign credit and work-permit income. Here's how they work.

body.heading: The three insurers and what they accept
paragraphs: Canada has three mortgage default insurers: CMHC, Sagen and Canada Guaranty. All three offer dedicated newcomer programs. CMHC and Sagen accept work-permit income up to 90% LTV (10% down). CMHC accepts permanent residents under 5 years up to 95% LTV (5% down) on insured mortgages up to $1.5M.

Foreign credit is accepted in all three programs — Equifax international, Experian, country-specific bureaus. Build Canadian credit alongside (a secured credit card on day one, paid on time) to expand options as you go.

body.heading: Down payment from outside Canada
paragraphs: All three insurer programs allow down-payment funds from outside Canada — but anti-money-laundering documentation is strict. You'll need: bank statements from your home country for 90 days, the wire transfer trail showing funds arriving in Canada, and a source-of-funds explanation if anything looks unusual. Family gifts from overseas need a gift letter and the family member's source of funds documented as well.
Plan for 30–60 days of advance preparation on this paperwork. Mortgage approval is straightforward; AML documentation is where most newcomer files stall.

body.heading: What about Canadian credit?
paragraphs: You don't need it to qualify under newcomer programs — but having it expands options. Apply for a Canadian credit card on day one in Canada (secured if needed). After 6 months of clean payment history you have a usable Canadian beacon score. After 12–24 months, you can graduate from newcomer programs to standard programs at slightly better pricing.

callout.title: Multilingual support
callout.text: I serve clients in English and Turkish. If you'd be more comfortable speaking Turkish for your file, just say so when you reach out.

faqs.question: Can I qualify on a closed work permit?
faqs.answer: Yes, in most cases. The lender wants 3+ months remaining on your permit at funding, plus stable Canadian employment. PGWP, LMIA-supported and Provincial Nominee work permits all qualify under the major programs.

faqs.question: Can my Canadian relative co-sign?
faqs.answer: Yes — co-signor structures often unlock better rates and higher amounts. The co-signor's income and credit are added to yours, often moving the file from newcomer to standard programs.

faqs.question: Will the foreign-buyer ban affect me?
faqs.answer: Permanent residents, work-permit holders meeting specific criteria, and several other categories are exempt from the federal foreign-buyer ban. Confirm with a Canadian real-estate lawyer before signing any contract.
